observations.managementAction: None.  As of Dec. 31, 2016 there were 2,007 job order employees as reported by the Human Resource  Management Officer Jose Balansag.
observations.reason: The hiring of J.O.s is a form of financial assistance to sugarcane farm workers who are unemployed during off-milling and post-planting season, and the fishermen who are greatly affected during the monsoon season.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management send confirmation letters to the different government agencies and corporations and private persons identified as debtors and to deduct from the salaries of employees concerned the receivable due from them.  Management should exert more effort in collecting the receivables.

(Reiterated under Part II of this report).
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Some debtors could no longer be traced due to death or transfer of residence while some are dissolved NGOs and former Disbursing Officers who absconded. There is also lack of manpower to dig up and retrieve the relevant documents.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that in the determination of the appropriations which may be considered pertinent to Peace and Order/Public Safety, only those appropriations which are to be utilized directly for the maintenance of peace and order and public safety as enumerated under Sec. 3.23 of Joint Circular No. 1 of COA, DILG, DBM, GCGOCCs and DND be included in the list in order to correctly compute the amount of Confidential Fund.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that the city government insure with the General Insurance Fund properties not yet covered by insurance amounting to more than P200 million in order that the city can be indemnified for damages to or losses of properties due to fire, earthquake, floods, storms or other calamities.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that henceforth, all collections made by any employee of the city government in connection with the city fiesta be treated as public funds, to be acknowledged by official receipts or cash tickets and remitted to the city treasurer for the proper accounting thereof.   Expenses shall then be covered by duly approved vouchers and subjected to the audit jurisdiction of the Commission on Audit.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that in the identification of projects to be charged to the 20% Development Fund, the guidelines and limitations under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 must be strictly observed.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that management immediately surrender its stock certificates to the manager of BACONSEPZ, Mr. Rebecco V. Ferraren, who is currently an employee of the city government, or to Mr. Ignacio Vicente, President, in order that it can recover its investment of P930,000.00.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended the immediate revocation of the office orders exempting officials and personnel from the biometrics timekeeping system, except for the ambulance drivers who are on call 24 hours a day, the Mayor’s security personnel and drivers as they keep the same hours as the Mayor, and the JO assigned to the Slaughterhouse.  We further recommended the reassignment of the eight JO watchmen from the Mayor’s and her son’s respective residences to the City Hall or other official premises, otherwise their wages may be a charge to their personal funds, while no wages may be paid to the two JO utility workers who were neither found at station nor known to the city hall personnel.  Lastly, we recommend a reduction to the barest possible minimum the number of JOs hired to drive the Mayor’s two service vehicles.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that henceforth, requisitions for fuel from the storage tank should be covered by duly-approved Requisition and Issue Slips and the quantity of fuel issued recorded or posted to the Driver’s Trip Ticket, to be accounted for until consumed.  We further recommended that the Driver’s Trip Tickets be properly and completely accomplished to show all the required information, including the names of passengers with their signatures, in order that the reasonableness of fuel consumption can be determined. We also recommended that management ensure that all vehicles are equipped with working odometers, and that the trip ticket form be corrected to conform to the prescribed form (Annex 17).

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that the City Mayor, as representative of the City of Bais, request the treasurer of the League of Cities of the Philippines to acknowledge its payments (as well as collections from other member-cities) with government Accountable Form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subjected to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that the City Mayor file a request to the Commission on Audit for the write-off of dormant accounts receivable following the procedures outlined under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.  In addition, we recommended that the city exert effort in the collection of receivables aged two to 10 years amounting to P1,817,468.88 to avoid loss of government funds.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that henceforth, OTS be remunerated through CTO and that remuneration in cash be authorized only in exceptional cases when application of CTO would adversely affect the operations of the city.  We further recommended that OT requests specifically state in detail the duties to be done, the justification as to necessity and urgency, the adverse effects if overtime is not resorted to, and that accomplishment reports be quantified.

RECOMMENDATION
We recommended that the use of the Kia 2700 Rescue Vehicle, Forland Mini Dump Truck and the Isuzu Water Tanker for ordinary, day-to-day operations be immediately stopped in order to preserve the utility of these vehicles to times of actual calamity solely for rescue and response activities in conformity with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014.

We recommend that the General Services Officer determine the persons accountable for the movable properties which could not be accounted for at the time of the physical count, who should be made to pay the value thereof as shown in the general ledger, and that the concerned department heads be required to prepare the Summaries of Supplies and Materials Issued, otherwise a determination should be made as to who should answer for the inventories which are no longer existent.
observations.managementAction: Reconstruction of Requisition and Issue Slips and preparation of SSMI are currently being done by the GSO in coordination with the City Engineer’s Office.  With regard to the unaccounted properties, demand letters have been sent. Reconciliation of the accounts is also ongoing.
observations.reason: The processes are tedious and cannot be completed immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the seller, Don Gaspar Vicente, Inc., to refund the amount of P7,833,429.81 representing the cost of the road lot and the Environmental Protection lot, both of which may not be the subject of sale pursuant to Presidential Decree No. 1216 otherwise, the loss shall be borne by officials and personnel determined to be responsible for the transaction which is grossly disadvantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction:
(Note:  On Sept. 15, 2016, the audit team issued ND No. 2016-001 disallowing the amount of P7,833,429.81.) 
On Sept. 27, 2016 a Deed of Exchange was executed by Don Gaspar Vicente Inc. & the City of Bais for the substitution of two lots and return of P1,081,953.87.  Thereafter, the mayor, through counsel, submitted an appeal memorandum dated Dec. 13, 2016. (The audit team’s reply thereto was submitted to COA R.O on Jan.27, 2017).

observations.recommendation: Accountant strictly implement the provisions of Executive Order Nos. 248 and 298 as to entitlement of per diems and the rates thereof, and that the City Mayor desist from authorizing certain employees to charge actual expenses incurred in official travel except only in meritorious cases and when justified by prevailing circumstances, the justification, terms and limitations of which should be indicated in the travel authority to be issued and which should be the most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was implemented immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the subsidiary ledger bookkeeper be required to first review the payroll amounts before posting the figures to the ledger, and to maintain more detailed T-ledgers for amounts due the GSIS on the credit side, the opposite of which would show the amount remitted for each employee on the debit side, thus making it easier to locate errors, underremittances or overremittances.  We also recommend that the existing balances of the subsidiary accounts due the GSIS be investigated and adjusted as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction: January to March 2016 has been reconciled. Reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor and Vice Mayor exercise prudence in the grant of authority to their personnel to travel on official business and to ensure that only those which are necessary, relevant and urgent are charged to public funds.
observations.managementAction: Recommendation implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant and the heads of Departments enjoin all officials and employees to observe COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and to enforce the appropriate administrative and/or legal sanctions provided under said COA circular.
observations.managementAction: Unliquidated cash advance balance was reduced to P3,591,157.16 at the end of the year (net of CIF), aged less than one year to five years.
observations.reason: No sanctions were imposed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Trust Fund Bookkeeper to trace the transactions and effect corrections in the trust liability accounts Due to BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made to one account, Due to BIR which showed a positive balance as of December 31, 2015. Negative balances of the other three accounts remained, totalling P1,622,762.72
observations.reason: The job-order contract of the personnel assigned to adjust the accounts was not renewed.  No other personnel has been assigned to take over except the regular bookkeeper who has a voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the personnel in charge of preparing the payroll to deduct from the succeeding month’s salary the value of undertimes incurred in excess of the vacation leave credits as certified by the Human Resource Management Office.
observations.managementAction: Value of undertimes is now being deducted from the monthly payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Treasurer augment and monitor the personnel assigned to sort, prepare and transmit the transaction documents to the City Accountant, who should endeavor to prepare and submit the required reports on or before their respective due dates.
observations.managementAction: Number of personnel was not augmented.  However, strategies were applied in order to hasten the submission of the reports.  The delay has been reduced to 3 to five months.
observations.reason: City could not hire additional personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management issue a written demand to Mr. Theodore Burgos or otherwise avail of means provided under the law to compel the concerned employee to return the city-owned properties.
observations.managementAction: A letter of demand was already issued to Mr. Theodore Burgos in CY 2013.  As of January 31, 2016  the unadjusted net book value of unreturned properties amounted to P86,808.66, to be deducted from his terminal leave.
observations.reason: The final amount of accountability is still being established.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Agriculturist supervise closely the Dispersal Program and see to it that proper records are kept and that all movement (birth of offspring, death, transfers) are completely recorded in their records and reported to the GSO and Accounting Offices.  In case of death or disposal, the program manager be required to facilitate the request for relief of accountability, or to require payment of the value of the animal if warranted under the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Agriculturist has started conducting a livestock inventory and profiling which is about 60% complete for the purpose of updating the data on the dispersed animals.
observations.reason: Distant location of animals and unfavorable climatic conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider other uses to which the site of the firing range, as well as the unfinished building intended as comfort rooms and storage, can be converted, in order to recover its construction cost amounting to P340,000.00.
observations.managementAction:
The Chief of Police submitted a justification as to the need.

The matter was taken up by the Sangguniang Panlungsod and it agreed to appropriate additional funds to complete the project.
observations.reason: Fund allocation was not sufficient to complete the facility

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our prior years’ recommendation that management require the Inventory Committee to conduct, prepare and submit, in prescribed form the Report on Physical Count of inventories and on Other Assets and every year thereafter in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We further recommend that the Accounting Office and the General Services Office be required to reconcile their records in compliance with Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs to ascertain the accuracy of the recorded property, plant and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Physical count has been completed and the inventory  report as of December 31, 2015 and every year thereafter has been regularly submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to trace the details and verify the validity of these payables, remit those with valid claimants, or send confirmation letters to creditors for them to submit their claims to the city government within a certain period of time, otherwise said obligations will be reverted in favor of the LGU.  For those without valid claimants, consider possible reversion to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction:
Reverted to surplus- P2,698,080.50;
Paid - P16,215.52
Other adjustments- P155,700.00 under JEV No. 1001210-0497 dated 10/31/12 and JEV No. 1001312-2373 dated 12/31/13.  
The payable balance as of Dec. 31, 2014 amounted to P4,992,337.19. As of Dec. 31, 2016, the balance was P4,980,693.42
observations.reason: The remaining accounts need to be reconciled first due to the presence of negative balances.  However, nobody could take on the task as the accounting unit is short of personnel.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Treasury, Accounting and Legal Offices to secure the Land Titles to all real properties as an indispensable requirement.  (Partially  Implemented in 2008 AAR)
observations.managementAction: Transferring of titles was completed but there was an error in the Lot Number.  Retrieval of the original certificate of title with an erroneous Lot Number is ongoing.
observations.reason: The personnel in charge for the retrieval of the original certificate of title had a hard time finding the document due to lack of central filing of the land titles owned by the LGU.
